block.title: Rundeck App Setup
block.text: If you are installing on a Forwarder or Indexer then the Modular Input stanzas will enable automatically when you submit the setup page.
If you are installing on a Search Head only , then the stanzas will not enable.

block.title: REST API Data
block.text: This app will poll several endpoints from the Rundeck REST API and index the returned JSON.You must be targetting a minimum API version of 18.

If you install the App on a Heavy Forwarder , the Modular Input (for polling REST Endpoints) needs to have access to your Rundeck index.This is because it executes Splunk searches to dynamically create endpoints to poll based on values in the search results ie: list of Project names.

block.title: Log Data
block.text: In order to get your Rundeck log data into Splunk you will need to setup a Splunk Universal Forwarder on your Rundeck server to monitor and forward the logs. More details are provided in the App Documentation.

block.title: Global settings
block.text: Enter Host in format : foo.myrundeck.com (don't include 'https://'). HTTPs is used by default to satisfy Splunk's Cloud Vetting requirements for secure network communications. You can also specify an alternative port with the host : foo.myrundeck.com:1234
The Rundeck Modular Input and Alert Action use Token Authentication. The token you enter here for the above host will be securely encrypted in local/passwords.conf.

If your Splunk administrator has provided a different index for you to use , type the index name or select from the list.Clearing the input field displays the full list of indexes on this Splunk instance only.If your indexes are in a remote cluster , you will have to type the index name.
Polling Interval in seconds or a standard CRON syntax.This can be overidden on a per Endpoint basis if you require different intervals for different Endpoints.
Whether or not to backfill the entire history or just start polling from now (local time)

input.label: Backfill Job Execution and Event History ?

block.text: All logs get written to SPLUNK_HOME/var/log/splunk/rundeck_app_*.log and are searchable in Splunk
